industries.name: Pyrotechnics & Flares
uses: Strontium nitrate (Sr(NO₃)₂) and strontium carbonate produce the brilliant crimson-red color in fireworks, signal flares, and emergency road flares — strontium emits characteristic red spectral lines at 606nm and 687nm when burned
Military signal flares, aircraft distress flares, and naval illumination flares use strontium nitrate as both oxidizer and colorant in pyrotechnic compositions
Railway and highway emergency flares (fusees) use strontium nitrate with magnesium fuel — produce intensely bright red light visible at distance to warn of road hazards
Tracer ammunition: strontium compounds in tracer bullet base composition produce the red trail marking bullet trajectory in machine gun fire and tank gunnery training

companies.name: Chemring Group
companies.use: Major defense pyrotechnics manufacturer producing strontium-based signal flares, aircraft countermeasure flares, illumination rounds, and tracer ammunition for NATO and allied forces.

companies.name: Alliant Techsystems (ATK / Northrop Grumman)
companies.use: Major US defense contractor producing strontium-containing tracer ammunition, signal flares, and illumination munitions for the US military and allied governments.

industries.name: Ferrite Permanent Magnets
uses: Strontium ferrite (SrFe₁₂O₁₉, strontium hexaferrite) permanent magnets — the world's highest-volume permanent magnet material by weight. Lower energy product than NdFeB rare earth magnets but vastly cheaper and corrosion-resistant
Strontium ferrite in small DC motors for automotive accessories (windows, wipers, seats, mirrors), household appliances (vacuum cleaners, fans, blenders), toys, and loudspeaker drivers
Ferrite magnets in loudspeaker drivers: the vast majority of consumer audio speakers use strontium ferrite rather than rare earth magnets due to cost — from earbuds to home theater subwoofers
Magnetic separation equipment: strontium ferrite magnetic drums, pulleys, and plates for removing ferrous tramp metal from food processing, recycling conveyors, and mineral processing streams
Strontium ferrite flexible magnet strip and sheet (embedded in rubber/vinyl binder) for refrigerator door gaskets, display signage, advertising magnets, and magnetic closures

companies.name: TDK Corporation
companies.use: World's largest ferrite magnet manufacturer. Produces strontium and barium ferrite magnets, ferrite cores for inductors and transformers, and NdFeB rare earth magnets for automotive, consumer electronics, and industrial applications.

companies.name: Hitachi Metals (Proterial)
companies.use: Major strontium ferrite and NdFeB permanent magnet producer. Supplies ferrite magnets for automotive motors, home appliances, and loudspeakers, and rare earth magnets for EV traction motors and wind turbines.

companies.name: Ningbo Yunsheng (Magnet)
companies.use: Major Chinese ferrite and NdFeB permanent magnet manufacturer supplying automotive, home appliance, and consumer electronics markets in China and globally.

industries.name: Glass, Ceramics & Specialty Chemicals
uses: Strontium carbonate in color television CRT glass: front panel glass containing 8–10% SrO absorbed X-rays emitted by the electron beam, preventing radiation exposure to viewers — largely obsolete as CRTs have been replaced by flat panels
Strontium in ceramic tile and sanitary ware glazes — improves gloss, surface tension of molten glaze, and reduces crawling and pinholing defects in firing
Strontium titanate (SrTiO₃) as a dielectric in multilayer ceramic capacitors (MLCCs) for high-frequency electronics, and as a substrate for high-temperature superconductor film deposition

Strontium aluminate (SrAl₂O₄) phosphorescent pigment: long-afterglow luminescent material used in glow-in-the-dark safety signs, exit markers, clock dials, and novelty products — stores energy from ambient light and releases it as green glow for hours
Strontium chloride in sensitive toothpaste formulations (Sensodyne) — strontium ions block dentinal tubules to reduce tooth sensitivity to hot, cold, and sweet stimuli

companies.name: Luminofor (LuminoChem)
companies.use: Major producer of strontium aluminate long-afterglow phosphorescent pigments for safety signs, emergency egress marking, and novelty applications. Supplies SrAl₂O₄:Eu,Dy phosphors to sign manufacturers globally.

companies.name: Halcyon Proteins / GSK Consumer Healthcare
companies.use: Produces Sensodyne strontium chloride sensitive toothpaste formulations — strontium chloride (10%) is the active ingredient providing dentinal tubule occlusion for relief of dentin hypersensitivity.

industries.name: Medical & Nuclear
uses: Strontium-89 (Metastron) as radiopharmaceutical for palliative treatment of bone pain from metastatic prostate and breast cancer — strontium mimics calcium and concentrates in bone metastases, delivering local beta radiation to tumors

Strontium-90 (⁹⁰Sr, t½ = 28.8 years) as beta source in radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs) for remote power stations and lighthouses; also in radiation therapy applicators for eye and skin treatment
Strontium ranelate (Protelos) was a prescription drug for osteoporosis — stimulates bone formation and inhibits bone resorption; withdrawn from most markets 2017 due to cardiovascular risks
Strontium-82/rubidium-82 generator system: ⁸²Sr (t½ = 25.4 days) decays to ⁸²Rb for cardiac PET imaging — hospitals elute the generator daily to produce fresh Rb-82 for myocardial perfusion studies

companies.name: Eckert & Ziegler
companies.use: Major nuclear medicine isotope producer. Manufactures strontium-89 chloride (Metastron) for bone pain palliation and strontium-82/rubidium-82 generators for cardiac PET imaging. Also supplies Sr-90 radiation therapy applicators.

companies.name: NorthStar Medical Radioisotopes
companies.use: Produces strontium-82 for Rb-82 cardiac PET generators using linear accelerator production — provides an alternative to reactor-based strontium isotope supply, improving US domestic supply chain security.
